title: Hide controller pointer lines during gaze focus
summary: 0.4.24 extends the existing CUA gaze-focus capture seam to suppress the visible controller pointer lines while the player owns focus, then restore the prior pointer state on release.
reasoning: The current player interaction model already uses gaze to claim focus. Leaving the controller beams visible during that focused state adds visual noise and competes with the screen-driven interaction flow, so the next bounded improvement is to hide the active pointers only while focus is held.

changes.summary: Snapshots VaM pointer visibility state and disables pointer lines while focused gaze capture is active.
changes.reasoning: This keeps the change local to the existing input-capture seam and avoids widening into unrelated control or Meta work.

verification: Stamp version 0.4.24 into player.version.json and BuildRuntimeInfo.cs, then build the player release in package-only mode.
Verify the packaged artifact is named FrameAngel.Player.0.4.24.var and contains the 0.4.24 asset, plugin, preset, and demo scene.
Verify the CUA player plugin compiles after the pointer snapshot and restore logic is added.
knownIssues: This slice only targets the currently exposed VaM pointer fields and line renderers; if any runtime still draws a beam from another component, that would be a follow-up seam.
Live VaM proof is still required to confirm the user-visible beam suppression in VR.
